description: Atoms are the building blocks of skills. Each atom represents a SINGLE effect. A skill can have multiple atoms. The skill_flow determines execution order and parallelism.

spawn.description: Instantiates a scene asset at a position relative to the caster.

params.asset: Path to the .tscn asset to instantiate.
params.offset: Position relative to the caster's facing direction (forward, right, up).
params.dimensions: Scale of the spawned object {x, y, z}.
params.duration: How long the object lives before being freed (seconds). Defaults to 0.5s if omitted.

movement.description: Applies continuous movement to the caster OR to a spawned object.

params.direction: 'camera' = player camera forward, 'caster' = caster forward, 'target' = toward locked target, or explicit {x, y, z}.
params.speed: Movement speed in meters per second (m/s).

push.description: On collision: applies a velocity impulse to the target body.

params.weight_multiplier: If true, force is modified by caster/target weight ratio.

damage.description: On collision: applies HP damage to the target.

params.element: physical | fire | ice | lightning | dark | holy.

skill_flow.description: Defines HOW atoms are executed — order and parallelism. If omitted from a skill, atoms execute sequentially in list order.

syntax.sequential: Atoms listed one after another: ["spawn", "movement"]. Executes top to bottom.
syntax.parallel: Atoms grouped in a sub-array: [["push", "damage"]]. Executes simultaneously.
syntax.mixed: Sequential steps can contain parallel groups: ["spawn", "movement", ["push", "damage"]].

skill_flow.default_behavior: If skill_flow is omitted, atoms execute sequentially in the order they appear in the atoms array.

hit.note: Spawn first, then movement starts, then push and damage apply simultaneously on collision.

hit_projectile.note: Same as hit — spawn, travel, then push+damage on contact.

skill_flow.engine_rule: When a step is a parallel group [[a, b]], the engine processes both atoms in the same frame. For push+damage this means both are applied to the target in a single collision event.

composition_rules.description: An atom that needs a spawned object applies to the MOST RECENT spawn atom's object.

defaults.note: If spawn atom's duration is omitted, engine uses 0.5s.
